topIssues.title: Docs are missing section on InteractOutside
topIssues.bodyExcerpt:
### 🙋 Documentation Request

It's exported alongside the other hooks, and seems intended as a public API, but is completely missing from the documentation. 

### 🧢 Your Company/Team

Quizlet

recentComments.author: rhysevans-qz
recentComments.excerpt: Probably using it similarly to how you use it internally: to handle a click or other interaction outside a target element that reliably works even as the react-aria library evolves. I'm working on fix

recentComments.excerpt:
I think this response will have some more relevant information https://github.com/adobe/react-spectrum/issues/9904#issuecomment-4219050595

The short of it is that `useInteractOutside` is considered p

recentComments.author: yihuiliao
recentComments.excerpt: Yeah we are aware that we are missing quite of few docs for our hooks, but we do plan to add additional documentation for them soonish

topIssues.title: Use `null` instead of `Number.NaN` in NumberField component
topIssues.bodyExcerpt:
### Provide a general summary of the feature here

Replace use of `Number.NaN` values in the NumberField component with `null`. This would also enable passing in `null` to the NumberField which was requested in: #5524.

### 🤔 Expected Behavior?

The expected behavior is that one must check for `null

recentComments.author: snowystinger
recentComments.excerpt:
If you can think of a way to do this and have it be backwards compatible, then we welcome a PR.

Otherwise, this statement holds
> Unfortunately though, I think this is going to need to wait for a maj

recentComments.author: ik-southpole
recentComments.excerpt: Hi, should we expect further discussion on this, or would you prefer a PR at this point? The fact that NumberField doesn’t accept null prevents us from displaying an empty input, forcing us to prefill

recentComments.author: snowystinger
recentComments.excerpt:
> as typescript has no NaN type

This isn't quite true, the type is `number`, which was chosen initially to reduce the need to declare state for the NumberField
```

topIssues.title: Make duration of indentation beeps configurable
topIssues.bodyExcerpt:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

In #18898, the length of indentation beeps was reduced from 80ms to 40ms. However, this can cause problems for some users, such as those using audio cues in VS Code.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Completing the work started

recentComments.author: CyrilleB79
recentComments.excerpt:
Thanks @SaschaCowley for elaborating a bit more, that was my main request.

So if we agree that there is no better solution than creating a new option, the question that comes is: where will this opti

recentComments.author: SaschaCowley
recentComments.excerpt: > I just wanted to emphasize the fact that when two groups of users encounter conflicting interests regarding a feature, the easiest solution is to add a configurable option so that each one can confi

recentComments.author: CyrilleB79
recentComments.excerpt:
@trypsynth, I am sorry that my previous comment has offended you.

I'll try to clarify my point. Initially we had 80 ms beeps, that were found to be distractingly long, especialy at high speech rates

The block `containerStart` contains the phase banner component but the guidance says to put that in the `headerEnd` block.

Given there are many more blocks now it may be that this ex

topIssues.title: The document of `lang` differs from the implementation.
topIssues.bodyExcerpt:
[`lang`](https://github.com/jsx-eslint/eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y/blob/main/docs/rules/lang.md) rule checks that a `lang` attribute on the `<html>` is a valid IETF's BCP 47 language tag and doesn't check whether `<html>` has `lang` attribute or not, but the document says `<html>` fails for this rule.

[

recentComments.author: ljharb
recentComments.excerpt: That'd be great, thanks.

recentComments.author: mehm8128
recentComments.excerpt:
That sounds good. If we don't remove `html-has-lang`, this change won't be a breaking change.
Could I create a PR to add an option that reports missing `lang` attribute on `<html>` element for `lang`

recentComments.author: ljharb
recentComments.excerpt: We could certainly add an option that fails if there's _no_ `lang` attribute.

topIssues.title: Table is not interactive on first click in SSR
topIssues.bodyExcerpt:
### Provide a general summary of the issue here

React Aria's table is not interactive on the first click when the page was loaded through SSR.

### 🤔 Expected Behavior?

The table should be interactive/hydrated on page load, not only after a user clicked on the page.

### 😯 Current Behavior

-

###

recentComments.author: florianloechle
recentComments.excerpt: We also noticed this issue today. We are using v1.16.0.

recentComments.author: rsolomon-muon
recentComments.excerpt: Anyone have any luck with a workaround? Right now we're forcing a re-render after client render, but combined with #8617 it makes any table-based experiences very suboptimal.

recentComments.author: greg-hoarau
recentComments.excerpt: I have the same issue with a Button in a Cell with RAC version `1.11.0`. It seems like react keys are changing on first click
```

{{ govukCheckboxes({
  "name": "t-and-c",
  "items":

topIssues.title: navigatorObject_toFocus doesn't send a becomeNavigatorObject event
topIssues.bodyExcerpt:
**Reported by dpeschel on 2013-08-24 00:54**
These methods all send becomeNavigatorObject events:
navigatorObject_parent
navigatorObject_previous
navigatorObject_next
navigatorObject_firstChild

but navigatorObject_toFocus doesn't.  This seems odd if you consider navigatorObject_toFocus as equivalen

recentComments.excerpt: cc: @wmhn1872265132, @hwf1324 maybe this is something you are also interested to look into.

topIssues.title: Using nested dialogs seems to depend on including a custom `<ModalOverlay/>`
topIssues.bodyExcerpt:
### 🙋 Documentation Request

I've been trying to use several dialog components nested inside each other.

I've noticed through trial and error that without a `<ModalOverlay/>` in the mix, this simply doesn't work at all: opening one dialog opens them all simultaneously, and once open they can't be c

recentComments.author: snowystinger
recentComments.excerpt: Noticed this is still an issue by opening our example in stackblitz and copying the example inside itself, including a reproduction https://stackblitz.com/edit/3vwpr2za?file=src%2FExample.tsx

recentComments.author: LFDanLu
recentComments.excerpt: This seems to be because we will only [conditionally render the nested Modal](https://github.com/adobe/react-spectrum/blob/6e57855257f54738418f98c37efae798bf9fb4ad/packages/react-aria-components/src/M

topIssues.title: Add react-virtual support
topIssues.bodyExcerpt:
Hi,

Could you add https://tanstack.com/virtual support, at least [optionally](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/configuring-npm/package-json#optionaldependencies)?

By being maintained by the lib itself, it would help with potential upgrades / breaking changes, while providing the benefits of it.

Pos

recentComments.author: silviuaavram
recentComments.excerpt: That would be a good idea! We could add it in the [examples repo](https://github.com/downshift-js/downshift-examples) and also link it in the docsite.

topIssues.title: Update useMultipleSelection docs and remove references to itemToString prop
topIssues.bodyExcerpt: The definition of `UseMultipleSelectionProps` interface suggests the `itemToString` prop has been removed in v9 of `useMultipleSelection` hook. I skimmed the hook's source code and couldn't find the prop either so I suppose it's not a bug in the type but the prop has been actually removed. The docs

recentComments.author: silviuaavram
recentComments.excerpt: Thanks!

topIssues.title: Dark mode contrast issue on docs page
topIssues.bodyExcerpt: When using the documentatin in dark mode, the results under the usage examples on the hooks pages are very light gray on a white background and becomes very hard to see. I’m happy to help with a PR if there’s agreement on the expected behavior or design direction. Here are some of them:

recentComments.author: silviuaavram
recentComments.excerpt: Hey @ayberkuman this is quite a pain, thanks opening the issue! Feel free to propose the improvement and create a PR, it will really help the docs.
